pattern_name: Influence fails when treated as politics—or when buy-in is externalized
description: Getting ideas to survive requires mapping exec incentives and timing, not manipulation; failed buy-in is owned by the pitcher, and exec calendars are strobe lights of fragmented attention.

supporting_objects.pattern_name: Run Toward Fear on Dual-Bad Choices
Failed buy-in is your fault not theirs
Simulate exec with AI before live pitch
Surveys beat instrumentation when they conflict
Kill projects to build exec trust
Hour-turnaround framework response to exec invitation
Pick up subtle exec feedback cues

core_tension: Good ideas die without empathy for stakeholder incentives; blaming execs prevents learning.
behavioral_signals: Copy-paste prompts from Twitter; output reads like newsletter not your company.
Weeks of narrative docs; no Figma or code until late; surprise at eng estimates.
Eng refuses estimate after Bolt demo; PM surprised.
Double emoji length wins Arena; researchers optimize wrong loss.
pressure_behaviors: Rare wrong medical or legal advice; no thumbs down.
New role; company pivot; brand or regulatory constraints shift.
Skeptical eng; design wants pixels; sales wants demo.
Stakeholder sees Bolt demo; commits roadmap date.
failure_modes: Catastrophic tail event; no prior test.
Homogenized roadmaps that sound like every other AI PM; loss of trust from execs who know your voice.
Endless PRD iteration in chat; no shared reference object; misaligned mental models persist.
Significant underestimate; trust broken in planning.
organizational_contexts: Growth plus PM plus trust-safety sharing labeled incident set.
PM with bespoke GPT project instructions reviewed quarterly with manager.
PM who ships clickable truth before debate spirals in abstract nouns.
PM who labels prototypes with known lies list.
Leader who interrupted report in meeting
adaptation_directions: Targeted outreach after high-stakes sessions; bounty for failure reports.
Shared starter template with required voice and constraints section; peer review of system prompts.
Bolt or Lovable handoff from brain exports; label prototype as question not answer.
Demo disclaimer slide; explicit non-goals on prototype.

pattern_name: Distribution beats product in consumer social

behavioral_signals: Named agent Sam with defined role
Always-on Slack; guilt about blocking time.
Risk/legal builds shadow IT with Goose
failure_modes: Agents create more review work than saved
Half-built demos shown prematurely; feedback noise.
organizational_contexts: PM calendar with prototype mornings defended by manager.
Pre-PMF startup with design founder sensibility
adaptation_directions: Executive pilot programs with ROI tracking
Team norm: prototype blocks sacred; async responses delayed.
Match expert tier to capability gap
Pair with 30-second onboarding focus for maximum signal

pattern_name: Cheap code shifts the bottleneck to judgment and verification
description: When implementation is nearly free, ideation explodes and QA/review becomes the binding constraint; organizations must invest in taste, evals, and automated verification—not more coders.

supporting_objects.pattern_name: Code cheap shifts bottlenecks to judgment and QA

core_tension: Generation is cheap; knowing what to ship and proving it's safe is expensive.
behavioral_signals: Enthusiasm trying every new tool; low workflow depth.
Simulated Slack swarms testing IT workflows
pressure_behaviors: Tool FOMO; retention churn on shiny AI tools.
failure_modes: Rote building without design; sitting in corner on low-impact work.
Blaming model for human deployment choices
organizational_contexts: New hire replaces paid task SaaS with vibe app—shocks team.
adaptation_directions: Invest in design/problem obsession; persistence through pain cycles.

pattern_name: AI as teammate reframing changes usage patterns
description: Tool framing underutilizes AI; teammate framing changes collaboration—after experimentation period, assign work like onboarding a new hire with accountability for outputs.

supporting_objects.pattern_name: AI as teammate not tool reframing

core_tension: Teammate metaphor overtrusts vs tool-only leaves value on table.
behavioral_signals: Repeated let me catch you up meetings; same questions in every staff sync.
Team reads AI news more than tickets
pressure_behaviors: Reorgs; PM transitions; vendor and tool switches.
failure_modes: Tribal knowledge walks out the door; AI gives plausible wrong answers from stale fragments.
Tool-only usage leaving value on table
organizational_contexts: Org where onboarding is read the brain not shadow me for a month.
AI app team in enterprise or startup
adaptation_directions: Doc owner rotation; stale-after-14-days nudges; GPT project pinned in team wiki.
Publish what actually improves apps checklist for leadership

pattern_name: Any agent should be able to act as coding agent
description: Generalist agents that can read repos, run tests, and ship PRs reduce tool fragmentation—but increase security and scope-boundary requirements.

supporting_objects.pattern_name: Any Agent Should Be Coding Agent

core_tension: Generalist convenience vs least-privilege security.
pressure_behaviors: People-pleaser AI agrees with bad ideas
failure_modes: Agent starts coding on vague prompt
organizational_contexts: Non-technical PM at Meta building side products
adaptation_directions: Code as lingua franca for agents

pattern_name: Career capital decays without reinvention every 2-3 years
description: Past employer brand becomes liability; reinvention is emotionally costly but survival requirement in AI transition—cycle compressing to months.

supporting_objects.pattern_name: Task Loss Not Job Loss Frame

core_tension: Brand opens doors short-term vs hunger and skills long-term.
behavioral_signals: Engineers must post what they ship; velocity as core value.
Coordinators cannot show code or demos; seniors overcrowd openings.
pressure_behaviors: Everyone starts vibe-coding competitor; 3-month PMF treadmill.
Hundreds apply per role; AI screens resumes.
Candidates from failed startups only; moral pressure to 'never quit.'
failure_modes: Optimization theater on landing pages while category passes; Frankenstein feature tweets.
Years of coordination experience discounted; age bias masked as skills gap.
Survivorship bias in hiring; staying in losing game for lessons.
organizational_contexts: Job seeker with public portfolio of AI prototypes plus metrics stories.
Airbnb/Brian never-relax gas pedal; Apple death march.
Individual knowledge worker in platform shift
adaptation_directions: Five-tool CEO rare but rising with AI
Minimum lovable product; influencer > paid social; community Discord.
Reskill publicly; target companies with AI product surface.
Chase winning environments when learning is goal.

pattern_name: PMs collapse into coordinators when build cost drops
description: When shipping gets cheap, PM work that only moves information between people gets automated or absorbed by engineers; the surviving PM must build prototypes, tools, and decisions—not run status theater.

supporting_objects.pattern_name: Build internal tools not status reports

core_tension: Coordination was the PM job; AI and eng velocity make coordination worthless without builder output.
behavioral_signals: Job posts want coding PM; interview no repo access.
pressure_behaviors: Eng culture rejects PM code; PM culture rejects eng titles.
failure_modes: Duplicate work; unclear DRI; slower than either solo hybrid.
Burnout from PMs forced to code
PMs resist building and become expendable
organizational_contexts: Team of product engineers with PM on discovery only.
Product org automating its own coordination tax
Large tech org shedding non-builder PM layers
adaptation_directions: Pairing model with shared metrics; hybrid track in career ladder.
Pair PMs with eng on tools
Move up to strategy or down to building

pattern_name: AI-first mandate is non-debatable at leadership level
description: Leaders declare AI-first operating models explicitly; debating whether to adopt wastes calories while pilot-only mentality leaves value on table—though safety/regulatory paths still need human gates.
core_tension: Mandate without workflow change is theater; mandate with bad pacing is unsafe.
behavioral_signals: Test count drops before AI test selection
failure_modes: AI on top of bureaucratic process
organizational_contexts: Enterprise buyer plus data partner plus frontier lab
Fortune 500 transforming from acquisition holding co
Senior product executive with decades pattern recognition
adaptation_directions: Process deletion OKR before automation OKR
Count only serious deployments in success metrics

pattern_name: Software moat erosion drives ecosystem and hardware pivots
description: When software copies instantly—accelerated by AI—moats shift to platforms, ecosystems, and vertically integrated stacks; patents and features alone don't defend.

supporting_objects.pattern_name: Software moat erosion drives ecosystem/hardware pivot

core_tension: Shipping AI software while claiming software isn't the moat.
behavioral_signals: Calendar full of trying new apps; roadmap empty.
Green dot obsession; anxiety when offline.
pressure_behaviors: New model drop weekly; influencer stack posts daily.
Cross-timezone pings at 6am; exec DMs culture.
failure_modes: Migration whiplash; lost context; no muscle memory.
Always behind; no roadmap thinking; resentment.
organizational_contexts: Small team standardizing on Cursor plus one backend.
adaptation_directions: Tool budget: one experiment slot per month; document why switched.
AI Mode as distinct surface for experimentation
Team charter for response SLAs; execs use email for true urgent.

pattern_name: AI speed creates review and governance bottlenecks
description: Case-study speed (e.g. Sora-like generation cycles) outpaces legal, brand, and design review—creating new organizational bottlenecks unrelated to engineering capacity.
core_tension: Generation speed vs institutional review bandwidth.
behavioral_signals: We are still learning used in incident postmortem.
Teams ship without workforce impact analysis
AI for good without internal review loops
PM brags about exec DM access.
pressure_behaviors: PR crisis; regulator inquiry; competitor publicizes your failures.
Exec prefers DM; culture of backchannels.
failure_modes: Injury metaphor becomes legal reality; brand collapse.
Surprise launch objections; team learns decision late.
organizational_contexts: Regulated-adjacent product with published eval criteria.
University-linked institute or platform company with public trust exposure
Healthy org with DM for social not strategy.
adaptation_directions: Publish internal test pass rates; external trust follows.
Embed human-impact review in consumer AI launch criteria
Ask can we move this to channel; leader modeling.

pattern_name: Irrational purpose beats money-maximizing defaults
description: Founders and leaders sometimes choose purpose, craft, or mission over economically 'rational' paths—especially when academia or conventional career ladders undervalue ambition.
core_tension: Investor rationality vs founder meaning-making.
pressure_behaviors: Board wants collaborative CEO on layoffs
organizational_contexts: Ben vs six professors + Jan Stoicka
adaptation_directions: Mission stress test in partner meetings
Default round size to ambition test

pattern_name: Research preview as default lowers cost of being wrong
description: AI-native teams ship almost everything labeled early with short cycles, trading polish for feedback velocity; preview branding is honesty infrastructure, not excuse for neglect.
core_tension: Speed requires public imperfection; enterprise trust still demands GA discipline.
behavioral_signals: Calendar full of alignment meetings; board does HRWA too.
pressure_behaviors: 17 product marketers need wins; risk/compliance expansion.
failure_modes: Permanent support burden from previews treated as GA
Brand damage from buggy unlabeled launches
Preview used to excuse quality neglect
Feature flags for 0.03 message delta; admin headcount grows faster than ships/sailors curve.
organizational_contexts: Slack thread @ regression thousands of hours; FedRAMP debate scale complexity.
adaptation_directions: Leaders create clarity on valuable work; resist empire hiring.

pattern_name: Consumer vision tempo vs enterprise research split
description: Consumer product playbooks favor founder taste and ship-measure loops; enterprise requires customer research and spec discipline—the same PM identity cannot serve both without explicit separation.

supporting_objects.pattern_name: Listen deeply but don't build requested features
No customer research for consumer products

core_tension: Listening deeply vs not building requested features; Rabois vs Spiegel.
behavioral_signals: Different advice to portfolio companies by segment
failure_modes: Enterprise sales culture on lab buyers
Building wrong vision without any signal
adaptation_directions: Iterative prototype from user pain not specs
Reject obvious buttons that harm social dynamics

pattern_name: Mission sacrifice beats local OKR optimization
description: In mission-driven orgs, teams deprioritize local metrics for company-level wins—accepting product-line ego hits when global mission demands it.
core_tension: Mission rhetoric often fake; local KPIs still drive behavior unless enforced.
behavioral_signals: Teams willingly delay Code for higher mission priority
Stakeholder anxiety and identity threat during transition
All swings no ops; or all ops no innovation.
pressure_behaviors: Investor wants new product line; core decaying.
failure_modes: Local team sabotage of company priority
Swing fails; core weakened; no floor.
organizational_contexts: Leadership team with explicit swing list and owner.
VP Product with PMs stuck between equal options
adaptation_directions: Scripts: I will X if Y continues
Train managers on boundary vs request
Swing postmortems; core regression tests on CURR.

pattern_name: Resume brands select for coasting; undiscovered talent has alpha
description: Prestige credentials anti-correlate with hunger in tight markets; work samples and non-obvious pools beat logo hiring—though screening cost is higher.
core_tension: Brand signals baseline skill vs hunger and slope.
behavioral_signals: Execs surprised by lack of ops
organizational_contexts: Scaling startup tempted by FAANG VPs
adaptation_directions: Red Sox model: homegrown core plus few free agents

pattern_name: Shed coordination layers, rehire AI-first builders
description: Hyperscalers cut broad headcount then rehire smaller AI-fluent builder-dense teams—structural recomposition, not cyclical layoffs.
core_tension: Survivor overload vs knowledge loss; rehire same profiles without behavior change.
behavioral_signals: PMs say we need fine-tune without data; eng sighs.
Smaller teams owning more surface area
pressure_behaviors: Vendor promises one-click custom model; compliance asks for explainability.
failure_modes: Wrong layer investment; months lost fine-tuning when prompt plus eval would suffice.
Rehiring same profiles without behavior change
organizational_contexts: ML platform team educating PMs on what fine-tune vs prompt vs RAG solves.
adaptation_directions: Decision tree poster in wiki; escalate to platform when layer unclear.
Only enter tunnel with clear unlock (collaboration, i18n)

pattern_name: Preserved design process becomes liability under eng velocity
description: Research diverge/converge cycles are too slow; design splits into execution support now and 3-6 month prototype vision—embedded support over phase gates.

supporting_objects.pattern_name: Design split: execution support vs 3-6mo vision
Don't Trust the Process—design process is dead

core_tension: Craft identity vs irrelevance; accessibility suffers without any process.
behavioral_signals: Block vs T framework in hiring
failure_modes: Big upfront research missing real constraints
organizational_contexts: Design team hiring in blurred-role AI org
Head of Design in fast-shipping AI org
Design org in AI-native product company

pattern_name: Engineers with product taste beat PM headcount expansion
description: End-to-end engineer shipping from feedback to deploy reduces coordination tax; PMs reserved for ambiguous, infra-heavy, or cross-functional blockers.

supporting_objects.pattern_name: Engineers as mini-PMs under two eng-weeks
Hire engineers with product taste over PM headcount

core_tension: PM-less chaos on big bets vs PM overhead on small bets.
pressure_behaviors: PM/design capacity squeeze from eng velocity
failure_modes: Inconsistent UX without design taste in eng
Isolated PM loses; coalition PM wins resource.
organizational_contexts: Engineer-led shipping org with selective PM
PM with informal coalition across functions.
adaptation_directions: Red line vs testable controversy framework
Comp bands tied to impact not craft

pattern_name: Platform access compounds outcomes more than individual brilliance
description: Choosing high-leverage platforms is career compound interest; don't confuse ability with platform access—luck plus preparation matters.

supporting_objects.pattern_name: Platform choice as career compound interest

core_tension: Merit narrative vs unequal platform distribution.
behavioral_signals: Leaders boast about ruthless firing without support.
Builders ship across stack with AI; fewer handoffs.
pressure_behaviors: Champion leaves for competitor; fire fast culture quotes.
Company mandates return-to-office for leadership track.
Legacy specialization resistance; trust in AI output.

Invisible PM loses promo to office neighbor; timezone penalty.
Title fights while work changes; quality regressions.

Distributed team with intentional in-person summits not daily office mandate.

adaptation_directions: Reflect on unkind moments; support people out.
Document decisions in public channels; fly to key summits quarterly.
AI as matchmaker for economic graph—underutilized frame.

pattern_name: Season-based planning replaces static roadmaps
description: In fast AI markets, planning horizons compress into seasons with explicit bets and kill criteria—not annual roadmaps that are obsolete on publication.
core_tension: Need for direction vs futility of long-range commitment.
behavioral_signals: Excitement at offer signature, dread at work content.
Founders say startup feels slower at first
pressure_behaviors: C-level offers; family programming; peer comparison.
failure_modes: Take role for LinkedIn post; stay years in misaligned work.
organizational_contexts: Airtable LLM map-reduce on sales calls
Operator famous for founder support rejects fancy COO roles.
Founder after big-co career (Outpace, ex-Tinder CPO)
adaptation_directions: Walks repeating mantra; build safe leader spaces.
Conviction decisions when N too small

pattern_name: Under-resourcing forces agentification and focus
description: Scarcity of headcount and budget forces teams toward automation and ruthless prioritization—constraints as creative pressure, not just deprivation.
core_tension: Under-resourcing as strategy vs excuse for quality collapse.
behavioral_signals: Many engineers on one agentic feature
pressure_behaviors: Board pressure to cut AI spend early
organizational_contexts: CEO hiring first C-suite at ~50–500 employees
adaptation_directions: Single-owner projects with explicit speed KPIs

pattern_name: Feedback velocity becomes the product moat
description: When build is cheap, the org that learns fastest from usage—tight feedback loops, short cycle review—outcompetes feature copyists.
core_tension: Shipping volume vs learning rate.
behavioral_signals: PMs defensive about auto-PRs from agents
Bookmarks hundreds of posts; repo empty; asks eng to prototype.
pressure_behaviors: Performance review asks for AI fluency; no time blocked to build.
failure_modes: Leader only reacts to biggest win
Imposter syndrome hidden behind curated reading lists; no portfolio of work.
organizational_contexts: Head of growth at multi-PM growth org
Head of fast-moving devtool with vocal power users
Harvard Lampoon, Emmy writers for creative evals
PM who tracks build-to-consume ratio in public.

Invest in matcher ML + reputation graph
Build buddy accountability; share failures not just highlights.
